sections.title: What is Analogy?
sections.content: An analogy identifies the similarity between like features of two different things by classifying the relationship between a pair of words.

sections.title: Two Approaches
items: Single-Word Approach — A pair of words + a third word; choose the word closely related to the third word in the same way as the first pair. Example: canine : dog :: feline : ____ → cat
Paired-Word Approach — A pair of words; choose the pair with the closest relationship. Example: mango : fruit :: ____ : ____ → rose : flower

sections.title: Techniques
items: DTR (Define the Relationship) — State the relationship using a sentence. Example: 'A frog is an example of an amphibian.' Find the pair with the same relationship.
Narrow the Relationship — If the relationship is too broad, be more specific. 'A club is used in hitting a ball when playing golf' (not just 'used in playing golf').
Substitute — Define the relationship, substitute the third word for the first, then find the fourth.
Observe Parallelism — All words in the analogy must follow the same grammatical form (noun:noun, adjective:adjective, etc.).
Don't Reverse the Order — Follow the same order as the given pair.
